steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to find a quotation from Acquah that explains why she used pointillism in "Exquisite." The answer must include (1) a direct quotation from Acquah and (2) a reason or purpose for choosing pointillism, not just a description of the technique or the painting's subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "In painting 'Exquisite,' Acquah applied pointillism to create what she called an 'illusion of movement'...": Directly quotes Acquah's stated purpose — creating an "illusion of movement" — and links that purpose to "Exquisite" and its dancing figures. Satisfies both requirements. ✓
  • Choice B — "Pointillism, the technique used in Acquah's 'Exquisite,' involves the application of small, distinct dots of color.": Defines pointillism and notes Acquah used it, but provides no quotation from Acquah and no reason why she chose it. ✗
  • Choice C — "In 'Exquisite,' Acquah uses a technique that she says involves 'extending dabs of color in the subject matter into the background and vice-versa.'": Includes a quotation from Acquah, but the quotation describes how the technique works mechanically, not why she chose it for this painting. ✗
  • Choice D — "'Exquisite' portrays Acquah's fellow Ghanaian women as she sees them: the 'unsung heroes of the Ghanaian Republic.'": Includes a quotation from Acquah, but the quotation expresses her views on Ghanaian women, not her reason for using pointillism. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
